findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add an AC (or extend AC3's proving command) that asserts each pointer target file exists at delivery time — e.g. `test -f docs/ticket-model.md` — so a missing S1 prerequisite causes an AC failure rather than a green-but-broken delivery.
evidence: AC3 proving: `rg -n 'docs/plan-review-gate.md|docs/mcp-reference.md|docs/ticket-model.md|docs/concurrency.md|docs/plan-review-criteria-guide.md' AGENTS.md` — checks AGENTS.md contains the string, not that the file exists
docs/ticket-model.md is absent from the repo file listing
Cached review finding: 'docs/ticket-model.md is named in the plan's de-risking section as a pointer target but does not exist in the repository'
Plan scope: 'Depends on S1 (already linked depends_on): docs/ticket-model.md must exist before AGENTS.md points at it' — but no AC enforces this precondition
findings.finding: The AC proving commands verify that AGENTS.md *contains* pointer strings (e.g. `rg -n 'docs/ticket-model.md' AGENTS.md`) but no AC verifies that the pointer *target files actually exist*. Since `docs/ticket-model.md` does not currently exist in the repo and depends on a separate story (S1), an executor who runs this story before S1 completes will ship AGENTS.md with a broken link while all ACs pass green. The dependency is named in 'Scope' but is not enforced at the AC level.
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria — AC3 proving command; Scope — 'Depends on S1'
scenarios: Executor claims this story before S1 is closed; all ACs pass (the rg command finds the pointer string in AGENTS.md); AGENTS.md ships with a broken docs/ticket-model.md link that returns 404 for every agent that follows it.
S1 is later cancelled or scoped differently; the broken pointer in AGENTS.md persists undetected.
findings.suggested_fix: Extend AC3's proving command to include existence checks for files that may not yet exist: `test -f docs/ticket-model.md && rg -n 'docs/ticket-model.md' AGENTS.md`. Alternatively, add a standalone AC: 'All docs/ files pointed to by AGENTS.md exist. Proving: `for f in docs/plan-review-gate.md docs/mcp-reference.md docs/ticket-model.md docs/concurrency.md docs/plan-review-criteria-guide.md docs/reuse-surface.md docs/event-schema.md docs/user-guide.md docs/llm-framework.md; do test -f "$f" || (echo "MISSING: $f" && exit 1); done`'

findings.finding: The Scope section defines deliverables as 'In: new AGENTS.md, rewritten CLAUDE.md' only, but the De-risking section adds two additional write operations — re-homing content to `docs/llm-framework.md` and `docs/plan-review-gate.md` — that are outside the stated scope. Neither the Scope nor the AC set has a criterion verifying these re-homing operations are complete, leaving them as uncovered work.
findings.location: Scope section ('In: new AGENTS.md, rewritten CLAUDE.md') vs De-risking section ('Two stowaways ... must be RE-HOMED ... to docs/llm-framework.md / docs/plan-review-gate.md')
findings.suggested_fix: Add to Scope 'In:': '`docs/llm-framework.md` (add LLM-agent-operations paragraph), `docs/plan-review-gate.md` (add completion-verification close-gate blockquote).' Add AC: '`docs/llm-framework.md` contains the LLM-agent-operations content and `docs/plan-review-gate.md` contains the completion-verification blockquote previously in CLAUDE.md.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add ACs verifying that `docs/llm-framework.md` exists and that `docs/plan-review-gate.md` contains the re-homed content (e.g. `test -f docs/llm-framework.md` and a `rg` for a key phrase from each stowaway).
evidence: AC4 verify step: `rg -n 'llm-framework.md' AGENTS.md` — checks only that the pointer exists in AGENTS.md, not that `docs/llm-framework.md` itself exists or contains the moved content.

'the LLM-agent-operations paragraph → `docs/llm-framework.md`; the completion-verification close-gate blockquote → `docs/plan-review-gate.md`' — described as deliverables but uncovered by any destination-file existence check.
ABSENCE: no AC of the form `test -f docs/llm-framework.md` or equivalent content check.
findings.finding: The described work of re-homing two stowaways (LLM-ops paragraph → `docs/llm-framework.md`; close-gate blockquote → `docs/plan-review-gate.md`) is a migration, but no AC verifies that the destination files exist and contain the moved content — only that AGENTS.md *points* to them. E1d requires verifying BOTH removal AND replacement for migrations.
findings.location: AC4 / 'Re-home two stowaways' in What section
scenarios: An implementer adds the pointer line to AGENTS.md and strips the content from CLAUDE.md but never writes `docs/llm-framework.md`, leaving a dead-link pointer — all ACs pass but the stowaway content is lost.
findings.suggested_fix:
Add to Acceptance Criteria:
```
- [ ] `docs/llm-framework.md` exists and contains the LLM-agent-operations content.
  Proving: `test -f docs/llm-framework.md && rg -q 'llm' docs/llm-framework.md`
- [ ] `docs/plan-review-gate.md` contains the completion-verification close-gate content.
  Proving: `rg -q 'close.gate\|completion.verif' docs/plan-review-gate.md`
```

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add AC proof steps verifying the stowaway target files exist and contain the re-homed content: `test -f docs/llm-framework.md` (and spot-check it contains the LLM-ops paragraph) and `rg -n 'completion.verification\|close.gate' docs/plan-review-gate.md`.
evidence: 'the LLM-agent-operations paragraph → docs/llm-framework.md; the completion-verification close-gate blockquote → docs/plan-review-gate.md'
AC only checks: `rg -n 'llm-framework.md' AGENTS.md` (presence of pointer in AGENTS.md)
No AC checks `test -f docs/llm-framework.md` or that docs/plan-review-gate.md contains the close-gate content
ABSENCE: no proof step confirms the stowaway destination files exist with the moved content
findings.finding: The Acceptance Criteria verify that AGENTS.md *points to* docs/llm-framework.md and docs/plan-review-gate.md, but no AC or proof checks that those target files actually *exist and contain the re-homed content*. If the stowaway target files do not exist, the pointers are broken and the re-homing goal is silently unfulfilled.
findings.location: ## What — 'Re-home two stowaways' bullet; AC item 4 ('llm-framework.md present as a pointer')
scenarios: An agent creates AGENTS.md with pointer lines but never creates docs/llm-framework.md; the AC passes (pointer exists in AGENTS.md) but the re-homed content is lost
docs/plan-review-gate.md already exists but the close-gate blockquote was never appended; AC passes silently
findings.suggested_fix:
Add two AC items:
```
- [ ] docs/llm-framework.md exists and contains the LLM-agent-operations content.
  Proving: `test -f docs/llm-framework.md && rg -l 'LLM.agent.operat' docs/llm-framework.md`
- [ ] docs/plan-review-gate.md contains the completion-verification close-gate block.
  Proving: `rg -n 'completion.verif' docs/plan-review-gate.md`
```

findings.finding: The 'In:' scope list names only 'new AGENTS.md, rewritten CLAUDE.md', but the plan also requires writing/appending content to two external docs files (docs/llm-framework.md, docs/plan-review-gate.md) to re-home the stowaways. These are unacknowledged deliverables in the scope declaration, and the revised ACs assert they are done but supply no proof command to verify it — unlike every other AC which has an explicit `rg`/`wc -l` proof.
findings.location: Scope section ('In:' list) and Revised Acceptance Criteria bullet 2
findings.suggested_fix:
Update Scope: "In: new `AGENTS.md`, rewritten `CLAUDE.md`, stowaway content appended to `docs/llm-framework.md` and `docs/plan-review-gate.md`."
Update AC: add proving line: "`rg -l 'agent.operations' docs/llm-framework.md` returns a match; `rg -l 'completion.verification' docs/plan-review-gate.md` returns a match; neither phrase remains in `CLAUDE.md`."

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Replace 'reviewer confirms the section ≤ 55 lines' in AC2 with a deterministic shell command that counts lines in the Git/Gerrit section, e.g. using `awk` to extract and count the section.
evidence: "Proving: rg -n 'CONTRIBUTING.md' AGENTS.md (≥ 1 match); reviewer confirms the section ≤ 55 lines."
'reviewer confirms' is not a shell command and cannot be automated or re-run deterministically.
findings.finding: AC2's proving command (`rg -n 'CONTRIBUTING.md' AGENTS.md`) only confirms the reference exists but not the ≤55-line subsection length; the plan falls back to 'reviewer confirms the section ≤ 55 lines', which is a human-review hedge rather than a deterministic in-session command.
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria — AC2
scenarios: CI/automated review cannot verify the subsection length; a future edit silently bloats the Git/Gerrit section past 55 lines without a failing check.
findings.suggested_fix: Add a proving command such as: `awk '/^## Git/{s=1;c=0} s{c++} /^## [^G]/{if(s)exit} END{if(c>55)exit 1}' AGENTS.md` (adjust the section header anchor to match the actual heading in the file).

findings.finding: The plan mandates re-homing two 'stowaways' (LLM-agent-operations paragraph → `docs/llm-framework.md`; completion-verification close-gate blockquote → `docs/plan-review-gate.md`) but specifies no fallback or decision rule for an executor when those target docs already contain conflicting/adjacent content — e.g. if `docs/plan-review-gate.md` already documents the close gate in a different form. The Revised AC asserts this as a criterion but provides no proving command and no protocol for the append-or-merge decision.
findings.location: De-risking results — 'Two stowaways' paragraph; Revised Acceptance Criteria bullet 2

findings.finding: The De-risking POINTER list references 'ticket-model→docs/ticket-model.md' as a pointer target for AGENTS.md, but `docs/ticket-model.md` does not exist in the repository (confirmed by directory listing of docs/ and search). An executor following the plan would write a dead link in AGENTS.md with no fallback protocol specified.
findings.location: De-risking results — POINTER list: 'ticket-model→docs/ticket-model.md'
findings.suggested_fix: Replace 'ticket-model→docs/ticket-model.md' with an existing authoritative target such as 'ticket-model→docs/user-guide.md' or 'docs/event-schema.md', or add 'docs/ticket-model.md creation' as an explicit dependency in the Scope Depends-on line.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Close (transition to `closed`) spike ticket `eastbound-bald-raptors` (1d75-89cb-f37e-482e) before or as part of this story to formally certify the PROVEN mechanism, OR soften the 'PROVEN / this spike is resolved' language to 'evidence recorded in spike ticket (open)' until the spike is formally closed.
evidence: Plan text: 'The `@AGENTS.md` import is PROVEN in Claude Code 2.1.211 (headless canary + official docs recommend exactly `@AGENTS.md` + `## Claude Code`); no symlink fallback.'
Spike ticket 1d75-89cb-f37e-482e status: 'open' (not closed)
Spike ticket description contains 'SPIKE EXECUTED (PASS, definitive)' and execution evidence, but the ticket has no completion-verifier signature and has not been transitioned to closed
Spike ticket 'preconditions_summary': {'status': 'pre-manifest'} — no signed attestation
findings.finding: The plan states the `@AGENTS.md` import mechanism is 'PROVEN in Claude Code 2.1.211' citing spike ticket `eastbound-bald-raptors` (1d75-89cb-f37e-482e), but a direct show_ticket probe reveals that spike is status=`open` (not closed, not signed). The execution evidence exists in the ticket description, but the spike has never been formally closed — so the plan's past-tense 'PROVEN' and 'this spike is resolved' language overstates certainty relative to ticket state.
findings.location: Design decisions (spike-validated) section — 'The `@AGENTS.md` import is PROVEN in Claude Code 2.1.211 (headless canary + official docs…)'
scenarios: An executor reading 'PROVEN' and 'this spike is resolved' treats the mechanism as fully validated and skips any re-verification, not noticing the spike ticket is still open and could be invalidated or updated
If the spike was executed against a canary build and the production Claude Code behaves differently, the open ticket leaves no formal gate blocking adoption of the unconfirmed mechanism
findings.suggested_fix: Add a step: transition spike 1d75-89cb-f37e-482e to `closed` (the evidence is already in the description; closing it formalizes the resolution and makes the 'PROVEN' claim match ticket state). Alternatively, add a `depends_on` link from this story to the spike so the open spike surfaces as a blocker.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Confirm that the dependency ticket (S1) actually creates `docs/ticket-model.md`, or add a fallback: either create the stub in this story, gate this story's merge on S1 landing, or replace the pointer with a TBD note until S1 ships.

Plan text: 'Depends on S1 (already linked depends_on): docs/ticket-model.md must exist before AGENTS.md points at it'
findings.finding: docs/ticket-model.md does not exist in the repository and cannot be confirmed as being created by the depends_on ticket (ticket 'S1' is not resolvable via the ticket system). The plan explicitly names it as a pointer target in AGENTS.md and states it 'must exist before AGENTS.md points at it,' but there is no ticket evidence it will be created by the dependency.
findings.location: POINTER list and Scope/Depends-on section: 'ticket model → docs/ticket-model.md (produced by S1)'
scenarios: Implementer creates AGENTS.md with a pointer to docs/ticket-model.md; the file does not exist at merge time; readers follow a broken link
AC#3 proving command (rg for docs/ticket-model.md in AGENTS.md) passes even though the target file is absent — a green AC masks a broken pointer

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Either (a) close the spike ticket `eastbound-bald-raptors` in the store (confirming it is done) AND add the specific auth/environment preconditions that were satisfied during the headless canary test (e.g. credential mechanism, Claude Code version, OS), OR (b) cite a stable, verifiable permalink to the official Claude Code docs page that documents the `@AGENTS.md` import syntax — so the mechanism claim is grounded in at least one independently verifiable source.
evidence: Plan quote: 'The @AGENTS.md import is PROVEN in Claude Code 2.1.211 (headless canary + official docs recommend exactly @AGENTS.md + ## Claude Code); no symlink fallback.'
Prior plan-review cache (d722fdcdc6df034b567e.json): 'the ticket eastbound-bald-raptors has status: open and assignee: null in the store'
Prior plan-review cache (9b4aa49b973e2890d86e.json): 'Auth/HTTPS preconditions for headless Claude Code execution are not stated'
Prior plan-review cache (9b4aa49b973e2890d86e.json): 'The official-docs citation (code.claude.com/docs/en/memory) and its verbatim quoted text are Unverified — the URL cannot be confirmed to resolve'
Evidence class: PARTIALLY-VERIFIED (spike ran and left notes, but ticket unclosed and docs URL unconfirmed)
findings.finding: The plan's central integration claim — '@AGENTS.md import is PROVEN in Claude Code 2.1.211 (headless canary + official docs recommend exactly @AGENTS.md + ## Claude Code)' — cites official docs at an unverified URL (code.claude.com/docs/en/memory). The quoted guidance and URL cannot be confirmed to exist or to contain the stated recommendation. Additionally, the spike ticket ('eastbound-bald-raptors') that constitutes the empirical proof is still OPEN in the store, meaning the mechanism claim rests on an unclosed, uncertified spike.
findings.location: Design decisions (spike-validated) section: 'The @AGENTS.md import is PROVEN in Claude Code 2.1.211 (headless canary + official docs recommend exactly @AGENTS.md + ## Claude Code); no symlink fallback.'
scenarios: An executor implements the thin CLAUDE.md pointer trusting 'PROVEN', ships it, and discovers that the @AGENTS.md import syntax is not supported or has changed in the version of Claude Code they are running — guidance silently disappears for all Claude Code users.
A teammate tries to replicate or extend the spike and cannot because auth/environment preconditions are undocumented.
A code reviewer checks the spike ticket and finds it open, legitimately questioning whether the mechanism was formally validated.
findings.suggested_fix: In the Design decisions section, replace the bare assertion with: 'Verified against Claude Code v2.1.211 (headless, authenticated via [credential mechanism]) in an isolated temp dir; see closed spike ticket eastbound-bald-raptors. Official docs: [stable permalink]. Evidence class: Verified.' Then run `rebar transition eastbound-bald-raptors ... closed` to formalize the spike result.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Clarify whether creating/editing `docs/llm-framework.md` is in scope for this story (or add it to 'In:' and to the Acceptance Criteria), and add a `depends_on` edge if a prior story is responsible for creating it.
evidence: "Re-home two stowaways ... the LLM-agent-operations paragraph → docs/llm-framework.md"
"In: new AGENTS.md, rewritten CLAUDE.md. Out: CLAUDE.local.md (S3), the shim/reference sweep (S6), and code."
No `depends_on` edge to a story that creates `docs/llm-framework.md`
findings.finding: The plan instructs the agent to 're-home' the LLM-agent-operations stowaway to `docs/llm-framework.md`, but the Scope section ('In: new AGENTS.md, rewritten CLAUDE.md') does not list `docs/llm-framework.md` as a deliverable, and no dependency on a prior story creating that file is declared. If the file does not exist and creation is out of scope, the agent faces a blocking choice: create a net-new doc (unscoped) or leave a dead-link pointer.
findings.location: ## Scope + 'Re-home two stowaways' bullet under ## What
scenarios: Agent reaches the stowaway re-homing step, docs/llm-framework.md does not exist, and must stop to ask whether to create it or skip the move.
findings.suggested_fix: Add `docs/llm-framework.md` to the 'In:' scope list and add an AC: `test -f docs/llm-framework.md && rg -q 'llm-agent-operations' docs/llm-framework.md`; OR declare a `depends_on` edge to the story that will create it.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Confirm `docs/llm-framework.md` and `CONTRIBUTING.md` (with full Gerrit recipe) exist or add their creation/population as explicit in-scope steps with dependencies, matching the treatment of `docs/ticket-model.md` (depends_on S1).
evidence: 'the LLM-agent-operations paragraph → docs/llm-framework.md' — plan re-homes content here but does not confirm the file exists or that creating it is in-scope
'defers the full recipe to CONTRIBUTING.md (verbatim Gerrit → ~215-230, which misses the target)' — CONTRIBUTING.md is assumed to exist with full Gerrit content; no creation step or dependency listed
Scope section only lists 'depends_on S1' for ticket-model.md; llm-framework.md and CONTRIBUTING.md have no parallel confirmation
findings.finding: The plan re-homes content to `docs/llm-framework.md` and defers the full Gerrit recipe to `CONTRIBUTING.md`, but neither file's existence is confirmed or its creation scoped. AGENTS.md will carry live pointers to potentially non-existent files, stranding readers.
findings.location: What section — 'Re-home two stowaways' bullet; Design decisions — 'defers the full recipe to CONTRIBUTING.md'
scenarios: AGENTS.md pointer `docs/llm-framework.md` 404s for any agent or human navigating the repo
CONTRIBUTING.md doesn't contain the Gerrit recipe, breaking the condensed Git/Gerrit AC

Then add an AC: `test -f docs/llm-framework.md && rg -q 'Signed-off-by' CONTRIBUTING.md`.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add a pre-merge step (or AC) that greps the repo for inbound references to the CLAUDE.md sections being removed (`rg -r 'CLAUDE.md' . --include='*.md'`) and updates or removes any stale cross-references.
evidence: 'do not restate them in AGENTS.md' — the content moves out of CLAUDE.md entirely
No grep/sweep step in the plan to find other docs or scripts that reference 'CLAUDE.md#session-logs', 'CLAUDE.md#llm-agent-operations', or similar anchors
Acceptance Criteria cover outbound pointers from AGENTS.md but not inbound references to the removed CLAUDE.md sections
findings.finding: The plan removes content from CLAUDE.md (LLM-ops paragraph, close-gate blockquote under 'Session logs' heading) but does not include a sweep for existing cross-references into those CLAUDE.md sections from other repo files, risking stranded inbound links.
findings.location: Scope section; 'Re-home two stowaways' bullet
scenarios: A doc or onboarding script references 'see CLAUDE.md → Session logs' and the section no longer exists after rewrite
CI or another AGENTS-style file imports a CLAUDE.md section that has been gutted

Notably: rebar.toml:3 ('See docs/llm-framework.md and CLAUDE.md for the close gate + LLM module'), docs/api-stability.md:25 ('the documented tool set in CLAUDE.md'), docs/architecture.md:65 and :233, docs/releasing.md:249, docs/workflow-engine.md:29, and docs/workflow-editor.md:160.

scenarios: After this story lands, CLAUDE.md becomes a thin shim. This is a scope-exclusion claim: the plan excludes ticket-model content from AGENTS.md on the premise that a canonical doc exists to point to — but the target is absent.
findings.location: De-risking results section — POINTER list: 'ticket-model→`docs/ticket-model.md`'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Replace the negative '(do not restate them in AGENTS.md)' prohibition with a positive content-boundary rule: state explicitly what AGENTS.md WILL contain for each stowaway topic (e.g., 'a single pointer line: `LLM agent operations: see [docs/llm-framework.md](docs/llm-framework.md)`') so the executor has an affirmative specification, not a pink-elephant exclusion.
evidence: Plan text: 'Re-home two stowaways wedged under CLAUDE.md's "Session logs" heading (do not restate them in AGENTS.md): the LLM-agent-operations paragraph → docs/llm-framework.md; the completion-verification close-gate blockquote → docs/plan-review-gate.md.'
T8(e) probes for 'instruction-locality / pink-elephant antipatterns' — a negative 'do not include X' instruction naming X is the canonical form of this antipattern.
findings.finding: The 'What' section instructs the executing agent via a pink-elephant negative prohibition: '(do not restate them in AGENTS.md)' immediately after naming the two stowaways. Telling an agent not to include specific content by naming that content is a classic T8(e) instruction-locality/pink-elephant antipattern — it primes the executor with exactly the text it should avoid, increasing the risk of inclusion. A positive content boundary (e.g., 'AGENTS.md carries exactly a one-line pointer for each of these topics') is both safer and more precise.
findings.location: What section — 'Re-home two stowaways' paragraph: '(do not restate them in AGENTS.md)'
scenarios: An executing agent reads the stowaway description, registers the content mentally to avoid it, and then inadvertently includes a condensed version of it in AGENTS.md — exactly as pink-elephant studies predict.
A future editor of AGENTS.md sees no positive content boundary rule and re-introduces the stowaway content, not knowing it was deliberately excluded.
findings.suggested_fix: Rewrite the stowaway instruction as: 'AGENTS.md carries exactly one pointer line per stowaway: `LLM agent operations → [docs/llm-framework.md](docs/llm-framework.md)` and `Completion-verification close gate → [docs/plan-review-gate.md](docs/plan-review-gate.md)`. The full prose from CLAUDE.md lives only in the destination doc, not in AGENTS.md.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Ground the 10-harness ecosystem claim: for each non-Claude-Code tool listed in the 'Why' section, add a cited vendor doc or known prior art (e.g. OpenAI Codex docs for AGENTS.md, Cursor docs for file conventions) — or qualify the claim as 'Claude Code verified; others by community convention' so the scope boundary is honest.
evidence: Plan asserts: 'AGENTS.md is read natively by Codex, Cursor, Copilot's coding agent, Zed, Windsurf, Amp, Factory, Jules, Devin, RooCode, and Cline — so one canonical AGENTS.md covers the ecosystem'
Design decisions section confirms only Claude Code was spiked: 'The @AGENTS.md import is PROVEN in Claude Code 2.1.211 (headless canary + official docs recommend exactly @AGENTS.md + ## Claude Code)'
eastbound-bald-raptors spike ticket scope: 'a throwaway spike in a scratch dir or worktree' testing only Claude Code; no mention of verifying other harnesses
No vendor documentation is cited for any of the 9 non-Claude-Code tools (Codex, Cursor, Copilot agent, Zed, Windsurf, Amp, Factory, Jules, Devin, RooCode, Cline)
findings.finding: The 'Why' section lists 10 agent harnesses (Codex, Cursor, Copilot's coding agent, Zed, Windsurf, Amp, Factory, Jules, Devin, RooCode, Cline) as reading AGENTS.md 'natively,' but only Claude Code's @AGENTS.md import mechanism is empirically verified (the spike). None of the other nine tools has a cited vendor doc, prior-art reference, or empirical probe in the plan. This is a cluster of unverified capability assertions that underpins the core 'Why' motivation ('one canonical AGENTS.md covers the ecosystem'); if several tools use a different filename/path convention or require per-tool shims, the ecosystem-coverage premise is materially overstated.
findings.location: Why section — 'AGENTS.md is read natively by Codex, Cursor, Copilot's coding agent, Zed, Windsurf, Amp, Factory, Jules, Devin, RooCode, and Cline'
scenarios: If Cursor reads a different file (e.g. .cursorrules or .cursor/rules/), agents running in Cursor won't get the AGENTS.md content, defeating the single-file premise.
If GitHub Copilot's coding agent reads .github/copilot-instructions.md rather than AGENTS.md, the 'covers the ecosystem' claim silently fails for Copilot users.
If OpenAI Codex reads AGENTS.md but only at a specific path (e.g. repo root vs. .codex/), the coverage claim may be partially correct but misleading without qualification.
findings.suggested_fix: Replace the unqualified list with a tiered statement, e.g.: 'AGENTS.md is the de facto cross-harness convention (OpenAI Codex docs, community convention for Cursor/Windsurf/RooCode/Cline); Claude Code bridges via @AGENTS.md import (spike-proven, §Design decisions). Other harnesses read AGENTS.md by filename convention — no per-tool shim needed.' Cite one vendor doc per tier (e.g. OpenAI Codex documentation naming AGENTS.md, Cursor .cursorrules docs noting AGENTS.md fallback if applicable).

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Qualify the multi-vendor ecosystem claim in the Why section with a caveat (e.g. 'reads AGENTS.md by convention or configuration per each tool's docs') or cite one verified source per named tool, so the rationale is not asserted as universally confirmed.
evidence: Plan quote: 'AGENTS.md is read natively by Codex, Cursor, Copilot's coding agent, Zed, Windsurf, Amp, Factory, Jules, Devin, RooCode, and Cline — so one canonical AGENTS.md covers the ecosystem'
No per-tool verification in the plan; no 'verified against docs' callout for any of the 11 named tools
Evidence class: UNVERIFIED (general training-data knowledge, not empirically confirmed per tool)
findings.finding: The plan asserts that AGENTS.md 'is read natively by Codex, Cursor, Copilot's coding agent, Zed, Windsurf, Amp, Factory, Jules, Devin, RooCode, and Cline' — a sweeping multi-vendor capability claim. Each harness's AGENTS.md reading behavior is a distinct integration surface (different CLI, different config, different scopes). None of these per-tool endpoints are individually verified against documentation or --help output; the claim is stated as established fact in the opening 'Why' section.
findings.location: Why section: 'AGENTS.md is read natively by Codex, Cursor, Copilot's coding agent, Zed, Windsurf, Amp, Factory, Jules, Devin, RooCode, and Cline'
scenarios: Some of the listed tools may not read AGENTS.md at the repo root (e.g. they may read only tool-specific convention files, or AGENTS.md support was added/removed in a version boundary), making the 'one file covers the ecosystem' rationale partially false.
A future reader of the plan takes the vendor list as verified fact and stops investigating per-tool behavior before integrating a new agent harness.
findings.suggested_fix: Change 'is read natively by' to 'is the emerging convention read by (or configurable to read in) Codex, Cursor, ...' and add a parenthetical '(verified for Claude Code via spike; other tools by published convention — see their respective docs)' to distinguish the confirmed integration from the inferred ones.

metrics.claim_path: no-llm/no-network (structural; the fast claim check is a local HMAC verify)
